response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer with a strong track record in TypeScript, Node.js, and application-layer performance, but the Principal Performance Architect role demands deep hardware-level expertise — SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, and deep neural network workload optimization — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His closest adjacent experience is writing low-latency DLang services handling high HTTP throughput and some hardware-adjacent work with robotic arms, but these are several abstraction layers removed from AI accelerator architecture and silicon-level performance analysis. The educational background (BS + ~14 years of experience) technically satisfies the minimum threshold, but the domain mismatch is fundamental, not a skills gap that could be bridged quickly.
strengths: Demonstrated low-latency performance engineering at Dunnhumby, optimizing systems handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and high-performance databases
Broad cross-domain experience spanning embedded systems, hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms at Altom), and system-level tooling — shows adaptability beyond pure web work
Long, consistent career (~17 years) with evidence of self-directed learning, open-source contributions, and technical community engagement (talks, workshops, conferences)
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or any silicon/firmware-level performance work — the core technical requirements of this role
No background in AI, machine learning, deep neural networks, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator workloads — the domain the role is entirely built around
Skill set is almost entirely web and application-layer (TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB, React, Ember.js), which does not transfer to the hardware and systems architecture work required here

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware architecture experience
No AI/ML or deep learning workload expertise
Low-latency and systems performance work at Dunnhumby
